subject: Project Management
title: Feature-Driven Development
description: A quick win: understand Feature-Driven Development.

hook.text: In 1997, Jeff De Luca faced a failing project with 50 developers in Singapore. He needed a system.

buildup.text: He organized work around small, client-valued features — each deliverable in two weeks or less.

discovery.text: Feature-Driven Development gave large teams structure without heavy process.

twist.text: FDD was one of the original Agile methods, but it never gained Scrum's popularity or buzz.

climax.text: Its idea — organize around features, not tasks — influenced every Agile tool built after it.

punchline.text: The method nobody remembers shaped the tools everyone uses.

quiz.question: What does Feature-Driven Development organize work around?
options: Small, client-valued features deliverable in two weeks
